stories: Good morning Sean, it's Friday, the twenty-second of May
Let me kick off with a space fact that's equal parts beautiful and terrifying
On the exoplanet HD 189733b, sixty-four light-years away, it rains glass sideways
Temperatures so extreme they vaporise silicate rock into molten glass particles, then hurl them horizontally by supersonic winds
Your calendar is clear until the afternoon - a bi-monthly catch-up with Ajit on Webex at one o'clock, forty-five minutes
Plenty of focused morning time before that
The Traffic Police have announced plans to use artificial intelligence to detect violations and automate issuing penalties - faster enforcement, fewer humans in the loop
Police also wrapped up a five-day island-wide anti-drug operation netting seventy-eight suspected offenders, including one sixteen-year-old student
Good evening Sean, it's Friday evening, the twenty-second of May, 2026
Here's a fun one to kick off the weekend: today is both International Biodiversity Day and Sherlock Holmes Day
Sir Arthur Conan Doyle was born on this date in 1859
It's Saturday, so a proper day off is on the cards
If the weather plays ball, the roads should be calling
Separately, the CNB concluded an islandwide operation between the eighteenth and today, arresting seventy-eight suspected drug offenders, among them a sixteen-year-old student
Globally, the big story continues to be Iran
Talks between Tehran and Washington are still running through Pakistani intermediaries, and there's cautious optimism about a framework agreement
